parentQuestion: Which of these would you NOT find in the cerebral cortex?

scaffolds.q: What is the cerebral cortex made of?
scaffolds.explain: The CEREBRAL CORTEX is made of GRAY MATTER — it contains neuron cell bodies, dendrites, and synapses. This is where computation and conscious processing happen.

wrongExplains.0: White matter is beneath the cortex. The cortex itself is gray matter.
wrongExplains.2: The cortex is neural tissue, not bone. The skull protects the cortex.
wrongExplains.3: The cortex is solid neural tissue. Fluid (CSF) is in the ventricles.

scaffolds.q: What structures are found in gray matter?
options: Neuron cell bodies, dendrites, and synapses
scaffolds.explain: GRAY MATTER contains NEURON CELL BODIES, DENDRITES, and SYNAPSES — the computational hardware where processing happens. The cortex has about 16 billion neurons.

wrongExplains.0: Myelinated axons are in white matter. Gray matter has cell bodies and dendrites.
wrongExplains.2: Gray matter is neural tissue, not bone.
wrongExplains.3: While gray matter has blood supply, it's defined by its neural components.

options: Neurons that only leave the brain
Local circuit neurons that connect neurons within a region
scaffolds.explain: INTERNEURONS are LOCAL CIRCUIT NEURONS found in gray matter. They connect neurons within a region, modulating and processing local circuit activity.

wrongExplains.0: Interneurons stay local. Projection neurons leave a region to connect distant areas.
wrongExplains.2: Interneurons are neural cells, not bones.
wrongExplains.3: Interneurons are a type of neuron, not blood vessels.

scaffolds.explain: WHITE MATTER contains MYELINATED AXONS — transmission cables covered in fatty myelin that carry signals between brain regions. It's the wiring, not the processing units.

wrongExplains.0: Neuron cell bodies are in gray matter. White matter has axons.
wrongExplains.2: The outer brain layer (cortex) is gray matter. White matter is beneath it.
wrongExplains.3: White matter is neural tissue. CSF is fluid in ventricles and around the brain.

options: Organized bundles of axons in white matter
scaffolds.explain: FIBER TRACTS are ORGANIZED BUNDLES of MYELINATED AXONS in white matter. Examples include the corpus callosum and internal capsule — they're highways connecting brain regions.

wrongExplains.0: Fiber tracts are axon bundles, not cell bodies. Cell bodies are in gray matter.
wrongExplains.2: Fiber tracts are white matter structures beneath the cortex, not part of the cortex.
wrongExplains.3: Fiber tracts are neural tissue, not skull bones.

options: In the white matter beneath the cortex
scaffolds.explain: Fiber tracts are located in the WHITE MATTER BENEATH THE CORTEX. They're deep transmission highways, not surface processing areas. The cortex (gray matter) processes; white matter transmits.

wrongExplains.0: The cortex is gray matter with cell bodies. Fiber tracts are in the white matter below.
wrongExplains.2: Fiber tracts are neural structures inside the brain, not in the skull bones.
wrongExplains.3: Fiber tracts are axon bundles, separate from blood vessels.

scaffolds.q: What's the difference between cortex and white matter?
options: Cortex (gray matter) processes; white matter transmits
White matter is on the surface
scaffolds.explain: The CORTEX (gray matter) PROCESSES information with cell bodies and synapses. WHITE MATTER TRANSMITS information via axon bundles. Different locations, different jobs.

wrongExplains.0: They're different structures with different functions and compositions.
wrongExplains.2: Gray matter (cortex) is on the surface. White matter is beneath it.
wrongExplains.3: The cortex is the outer layer. White matter is beneath the cortex.

scaffolds.q: What would you find IN the cerebral cortex?
scaffolds.explain: The cortex contains CELL BODIES, DENDRITES, and INTERNEURONS — all gray matter components. Fiber tracts are white matter structures found BENEATH the cortex.

wrongExplains.0: Fiber tracts are white matter structures, not found in the cortex (gray matter).
wrongExplains.2: Myelinated axons dominate white matter. The cortex has cell bodies and synapses.
wrongExplains.3: The cortex is neural tissue, not bone.

scaffolds.q: Which of these would you NOT find in the cerebral cortex?
scaffolds.explain: FIBER TRACTS would NOT be found in the cortex. The cortex has cell bodies, dendrites, and interneurons (all gray matter). Fiber tracts are WHITE MATTER structures deep to the cortex.

wrongExplains.0: Neuron cell bodies are abundant in the cortex — about 16 billion of them.
wrongExplains.1: Dendrites are definitely in the cortex — they receive synaptic inputs.
wrongExplains.3: Interneurons are in the cortex — they modulate local circuit activity.
